speaker: narrator
text: Complete Integrated Draft - All Acts

speaker: narrator
text: Dawn bled into the Deep Hall as the lichen shifted from bruise-blue to silver-green. With the light came the low sound -- felt first in teeth, then in boot-soles, then up through the ribs.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: A breath-long pause -- did you imagine it? -- then:

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: The ward-line held under Serana's palm -- chalk geometry and nine-stroke precision, the sigils she'd drawn before sleep still bright enough to matter. They stole some of the bass out of the air, damping the pulse like thick velvet over a drum. Not all of it. Not anymore.

speaker: narrator
text: Scrape from the forge corridor. Tap-tap at the sealed mine. A wet drag through floodwater that should have been still for centuries. The mountain answered with another dull knock that belonged to no mortal heart.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin woke to that slow count and the smell of old smoke and cold iron. The scale in his forearm held its coal-glow, the pulse tightened -- not warning, not ease -- like a hand at the small of his back urging him forward.

speaker: narrator
text: The familiar ache had settled in overnight to its hateful normal. He flexed carefully. He could manage small work today -- if he paid every time.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana kept her hand on the runes. No prayer came. Nothing answered. The chalk held because chalk didn't need permission -- only ratios and repetition and the stubbornness of someone who hadn't stopped yet.

speaker: unknown
text: "They've ringed the entrances,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, voice low and steady.

speaker: unknown
text: "We don't wait to be tested to failure. We choose our ground."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex was already up, knives in hand, standing where the Illusion alcove threw tricky reflections into the corners.

speaker: unknown
text: "Fast careful,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: unknown
text: "And if that's impossible, just fast."

speaker: narrator
text: She glanced at Caelin's arm.

speaker: unknown
text: "Beacon-boy, if you could stop being a lighthouse -- "

speaker: unknown
text: "Working on it,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, and because the truth cost less than a lie, added,

speaker: unknown
text: "I can't."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik had grandfather's journal spread on the black table, brass goggles riding his brow, big fingers unshakably precise. His beard smelled faintly of beeswax oil and iron dust.

speaker: unknown
text: "Forge Road's our best way out -- old ore route, still marked sound when Grandfather walked it. Six hours if stone loves us, twelve if it doesn't."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen sat cross-legged near the Abjuration alcove where her vine-ink picked up a little of the ward-light, emerald threaded with silver-gold. She lay her palm to the floor and winced -- stone fever, pressure, the fabric of the place pulled too tight.

speaker: unknown
text: "This chamber holds for now,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: unknown
text: "Everything tied to the sanctum's bones is failing. Whatever hunts us is... patient. Learning the ward geometry."

speaker: narrator
text: A smear of darkness slid under the rune-line at her feet -- a thin filament of shadow testing the gap between two chiselled glyphs. The ward flared white-hot.

speaker: narrator
text: For one knife-bright instant the shadow became a living silhouette of its owner's hand -- tendons, knuckles, too many joints -- then ignited.

speaker: narrator
text: It fled screaming without a sound, scuttling back as a scatter of ash that ran like insects, burned to nothing before it reached the dark.

speaker: narrator
text: No one breathed.

speaker: nyxara
text: "They're clever,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said softly from the Necromancy alcove, as if admiring a well-played move in someone else's game. Her purple eyes tracked the ward seam a beat longer.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Not clever enough."

speaker: narrator
text: On the edge of the chamber Durgan stood with his back to stone, eyes pale and unblinking. His shadow lay neat at his boots. It twitched once toward the ward-line when the burned ash fled, and he crushed it flat until his jaw clicked.

speaker: narrator
text: The pressure of holding it down built behind his eyes like fingers pressing into his temples. When he finally released his concentration, a thin warmth trickled from his left nostril. He wiped it with the back of his hand -- checking, confirming. The skin came away with a single red smear.

speaker: narrator
text: First blood, he thought, and tucked his hand away before anyone could see. How many more before I can't stop it anymore?

speaker: serana
text: "Move before they learn a new trick,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said. She pushed to her feet and didn't look back to check the wards. They held or they didn't. Either way, the only answer was forward.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex rolled a shoulder, felt the pull of healing ribs beneath.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Define intact."

speaker: serana
text: "Alive,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Everything else is optional."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik snapped the journal shut and slung on his pack.

speaker: serana
text: "Forge Road starts through the transmutation arch. Watch your steps. The mountain is currently... structurally questionable but functional."

speaker: vex
text: "That phrase has never comforted me,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: thornik
text: "It wasn't supposed to,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik replied cheerfully.

speaker: vex
text: "It's supposed to be true."

speaker: narrator
text: They packed to travel-light: water, rope, wedges and braces for Thornik, rations like stone, no spare comforts. The ward-line pulsed off-beat once, a tired strobe. The bass in the room thickened.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: serana
text: "Go,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said.

speaker: narrator
text: They went.

speaker: narrator
text: Forge Road fell away steeply, steps cut straight into living rock by hands that valued efficiency. The air turned wet and metallic; the tunnel smelled of wet granite, old slag, and the copper tang of slow rust. Thornik's lantern burned low, the flame cupped, throwing a disciplined cone of light.

speaker: narrator
text: He read the stone as he walked -- hairline fractures, load paths, the cross-rib where an ancient brace took more weight than it should. He laid spring-steel wedges into seams with a quiet click, braces kissing the stone to buy them minutes later when seconds might matter.

speaker: vex
text: "Don't touch the walls,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: serana
text: "They're listening."

speaker: narrator
text: The sound from below grew clearer as the wards' dampening effect faded with distance. No longer muffled. Present. Rhythmic.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: An hour in, the mountain shivered. Dust fell in silky threads. Everyone stopped on instinct, breath held until the tremor passed.

speaker: serana
text: "Quickly,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, and they did.

speaker: narrator
text: They came to a collapse -- a doorway half-buried by a spill of dwarf-cut granite. Thornik knelt.

speaker: vex
text: "Option one: explosive. Brings the ceiling to visit. Option two: levers. Time we don't have."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin stepped beside him, put his right hand on the keystone, and called the smallest heat he could bear -- a focused candle on a pinhead, nothing grand. The scale warmed; the ache sharpened like a pulled muscle. Granite ticked and softened to a bead of glass the crowbar could bite.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik levered. Stone slid. Passage opened.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin pulled back, breath hissing. The filaments flared angry red, then settled back to baseline coal-glow. He didn't count what he had left. He didn't know. He only knew there was still enough to hurt and not enough to be careless.

speaker: nyxara
text: "You know,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said as she followed him through,

speaker: vex
text: "most men would have tried a bonfire to impress me."

speaker: caelin
text: "I'm rationing,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: vex
text: "Tragic. I had a speech about stamina prepared."

speaker: narrator
text: He looked at her.

speaker: caelin
text: "Save it for someone persuadable."

speaker: narrator
text: A narrow twitch at the corner of her mouth -- caught between miffed and entertained.

speaker: vex
text: "You're going to be the end of me, Ember-boy."

speaker: vex
text: "Get in line,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex called from the far side, one hand pressed to her ribs as if checking they still held.

speaker: caelin
text: "We're all waiting our turn."

speaker: narrator
text: The mountain answered with a deeper thud that rattled loose grit down Caelin's neck.

speaker: narrator
text: They moved on.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: A long corridor followed -- clean, quiet, no falls, no scrapes -- only the heartbeat below growing clearer as the wards' bass-trap effect faded with distance. The sound no longer felt distant.

speaker: narrator
text: It felt present -- not beneath them specifically, but everywhere at once, resonating through the mountain like a heartbeat felt through pressed palms.

speaker: narrator
text: In the hush, a shadow-dart whispered along the floor ahead of them, a hair-thin ribbon sliding fast.

speaker: narrator
text: It struck an old dwarven inlay they barely saw -- a sliver of ward rune hidden in decorative filigree -- and the floor line erupted in a curtain of sheet-fire two hands high.

speaker: narrator
text: The dart became a coil of screaming soot-snakes that fled backward into the dark.

speaker: vex
text: "Good,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex murmured.

speaker: caelin
text: "Let it keep proving we shouldn't turn around."

speaker: narrator
text: Two hours in, they crawled through a chamber where the ceiling had molted down blocks the size of coffins.

speaker: thornik
text: "Fast careful,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said.

speaker: caelin
text: "Step where I step. Don't even breathe loudly."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's boot knocked a small stone. It skittered. A support block shifted a fraction, and the chamber groaned like a sleeping giant turning over. They sprinted the last ten feet in a pelting rain of grit and pea-stone. Not a full collapse. A reminder.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them, the pursuit-scrapes paused. Listening. Counting. Herding.

speaker: durgan
text: "They want us deeper,"

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan said without looking back. His voice was flat, factual.

speaker: serana
text: "Then we go deeper on our terms,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana answered.

speaker: narrator
text: Three hours in, Elowen stopped with her hand to the wall. The rootwork in her arms brightened; a single vine-filament along her wrist blackened another shade, flaking an ash petal onto the floor.

speaker: durgan
text: "The Weave is screaming ahead. A major brace near a junction is under more stress than it can carry. If it goes while we're under it -- "

speaker: serana
text: "How long?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana asked.

speaker: durgan
text: "Minutes. Hours. Or it started while I was speaking."

speaker: vex
text: "Faster,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, and winced as her ribs protested the thought.

speaker: narrator
text: They went faster.

speaker: narrator
text: Four hours in, another choke point. Thornik measured. Levers would work -- eventually. Explosives would kill them. Caelin set his palm to a stone and gave it heat like a match-head. Glass bloomed under his hand; Thornik heaved; the way opened.

speaker: narrator
text: Pain threaded Caelin's lattice, not a scream, a serrated sigh. The scale pulsed angry red for three heartbeats, then faded back to coal-glow. Manageable, for now.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara fell in beside him again, voice a ribbon of dark amusement.

speaker: durgan
text: "Refreshing, this restraint. I've been tracking something hot all morning, but I suppose I can exercise patience."

speaker: caelin
text: "Tracking the path,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: narrator
text: Her eyes glinted.

speaker: durgan
text: "Among other things."

speaker: caelin
text: "Save those speeches too,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, and kept moving.

speaker: narrator
text: A laugh. Low. Honest. Then the heartbeat underfoot stole the humor back.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Five hours in, Serana held a thin, mobile ward over them to shed small falls and carry the sound of their feet away. It flickered once, a brief stutter that she forced flat. She didn't meet anyone's eyes.

speaker: vex
text: "Define 'fine,'"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: serana
text: "Not dying,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said.

speaker: vex
text: "Optional,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex muttered.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's breath rasped under his cheer.

speaker: serana
text: "We're close to the junction chambers,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: vex
text: "Three-way split: mines, smelters, caldera to surface. The caldera stair is our goal."

speaker: vex
text: "Unless the mountain disagrees,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: thornik
text: "Stone always gets a vote,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said.

speaker: vex
text: "Today I'm asking it nicely."

speaker: narrator
text: Six hours in, the tunnel widened without warning.

speaker: narrator
text: They swung into a vast chamber where the walls curved like the inside of a bell.

speaker: narrator
text: Bridges of black iron spanned the emptiness -- hundreds of them -- some whole, some snapped mid-arch and hanging by cables older than language. Below, molten seams traced geometric rivers through the dark, glowing like veins under translucent skin.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's breath fogged his goggles.

speaker: thornik
text: "This isn't natural construction. It's metallurgy on a planetary scale."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara's voice echoed softly:

speaker: vex
text: "Or worship with better tools."

speaker: narrator
text: The Chamber of Echoes.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana held her torch close to inscriptions along the nearest bridge rail -- spiral glyphs carved deep and inlaid with gold now blackened by heat. Caelin brushed one with his gloved fingers. It thrummed faintly under the touch, familiar in the way of fever dreams.

speaker: thornik
text: "Same pattern as the Seal,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik nodded.

speaker: vex
text: "Then we're standing in its bloodstream."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex looked down into the molten rivers far below.

speaker: thornik
text: "Let's try not to puncture anything vital."

speaker: narrator
text: The hum shifted mid-sentence -- Thornik's last word echoed a heartbeat later, repeated perfectly but stretched thin, like parchment under strain.

speaker: narrator
text: Then Caelin's breath followed, an exhale caught and replayed back to him, slightly off-tempo.

speaker: narrator
text: They all stopped.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana whispered,

speaker: serana
text: "Echo."

speaker: narrator
text: But the word came back not once, not twice, but in every voice in the group -- each one slightly wrong. Serana, Vex, Nyxara, Caelin, Thornik, Durgan, Elowen -- all overlapping, all whispering echo in a chorus that didn't belong to any of them.

speaker: narrator
text: The sound faded like a tide withdrawing.

speaker: narrator
text: No one spoke for a full minute after.

speaker: narrator
text: They moved quickly across one of the intact bridges, giving the central darkness wide berth. The air here tasted of coin and ozone. Light from their torches bled into the rock instead of reflecting, as if the stone drank it.

speaker: narrator
text: Halfway across, Elowen tilted her head.

speaker: thornik
text: "Do you hear them?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana frowned.

speaker: serana
text: "Just echoes."

speaker: elowen
text: "No,"

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen said.

speaker: serana
text: "They're trying to remember what they were saying."

speaker: narrator
text: The air quivered. A dozen faint voices whispered over one another, too faint to catch -- then aligned, forming a single phrase in Draconic that Caelin understood without translation.

speaker: elowen
text: "Hold the flame. Guard the law. Bury the cost."

speaker: narrator
text: The same three lines, repeating in different cadences, layered until they blurred into music.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale in his arm began to glow faintly through his sleeve -- a bright warm pulse of recognition.

speaker: serana
text: "I've heard this before,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's face tightened.

speaker: elowen
text: "Prophecy?"

speaker: caelin
text: "Command,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin whispered.

speaker: elowen
text: "But it doesn't know who to give it to."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik produced a tuning rod from his pack -- a spare, cruder than the one the mountain had shattered two days ago, but it would serve -- ignoring Vex's muttered warning.

speaker: caelin
text: "If it's an acoustic record, we can pull more data."

speaker: narrator
text: He struck the rod lightly against stone.

speaker: narrator
text: The resulting tone spiraled outward, repeating itself, climbing octaves until the air vibrated visibly. The chamber answered.

speaker: narrator
text: A column of sound formed in the air -- dense enough to distort the light -- and within it flickered outlines of figures at work: massive shapes, part dragon, part machine, hammering something that shone like molten sunrise.

speaker: narrator
text: Then the image stuttered and bled away, leaving silence so heavy it felt deliberate.

speaker: narrator
text: A fine mist of dust fell from the ceiling, glowing faintly.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen looked up in alarm.

speaker: elowen
text: "Stop. It's -- "

speaker: narrator
text: Too late. The sound collapsed inward, a sonic implosion that slammed everyone to their knees. Ears rang. Torches snuffed.

speaker: narrator
text: When Caelin's vision cleared, Thornik was bleeding from the nose, staring dazed at his shattered rod.

speaker: caelin
text: "Note,"

speaker: narrator
text: he croaked.

speaker: elowen
text: "Don't do that again."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex kicked the broken metal off the platform, grimacing at the pull in her ribs.

speaker: caelin
text: "Brilliant deduction."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara stood very still, eyes half-lidded as if listening inward.

speaker: elowen
text: "They aren't ghosts,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said finally.

speaker: caelin
text: "They're instructions. The mountain replays them when something familiar enters the system. We woke the archive."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana drew a slow breath.

speaker: elowen
text: "An archive of what?"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara met her gaze.

speaker: caelin
text: "Every order the gods ever gave their creations. Every one that failed."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed once -- then again, brighter. The heat licked up Caelin's arm, across his collarbone, behind his eyes. Voices flooded in -- thousands, layered, overlapping. Command structures, prayers, screams of obedience and defiance all at once.

speaker: narrator
text: For a second, he saw through their memories: dragons kneeling to unseen figures, humans bound by glowing chains, cities forged then unmade by song.

speaker: narrator
text: He staggered, gripping the nearest railing.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen's hand found his shoulder.

speaker: elowen
text: "Stay here,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said fiercely.

speaker: caelin
text: "Stay present."

speaker: narrator
text: He breathed through it, the images fading like cooling iron.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana caught his other arm, voice sharp.

speaker: elowen
text: "Caelin!"

speaker: caelin
text: "I'm fine,"

speaker: narrator
text: he lied. The scale flickered white-hot for an instant -- lie detected -- then settled back.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik, pale but upright, whispered,

speaker: elowen
text: "It recognized him. The system thinks he's one of them."

speaker: vex
text: "Then we move,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: elowen
text: "Before it decides to collect."

speaker: narrator
text: They crossed the chamber quickly, their footsteps echoing in unnatural sync. Each time the echoes came back, they were half a beat too slow -- as if something behind them was learning to walk.

speaker: narrator
text: Near the far archway, the air shimmered. At first it was just heat distortion from below. Then it wasn't.

speaker: narrator
text: A voice said,

speaker: vex
text: "Caelin."

speaker: narrator
text: He froze. The sound came from behind -- his own tone, precise down to the hesitation between syllables.

speaker: narrator
text: Everyone turned. The path behind them was empty.

speaker: vex
text: "Not funny,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said automatically, though no one had spoken.

speaker: caelin
text: "I didn't -- "

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin began, but stopped. His throat had gone dry.

speaker: narrator
text: The voice came again, softer.

speaker: vex
text: "Caelin. Follow."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik muttered a formula under his breath, trying to anchor the acoustics.

speaker: caelin
text: "Reflected sound... but there's no source."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara's smile was faint.

speaker: vex
text: "Oh, there's a source. It just borrowed your mouth to speak."

speaker: narrator
text: The echo shifted targets.

speaker: caelin
text: "Elowen."

speaker: narrator
text: Her own name, spoken like a sigh through leaves.

speaker: narrator
text: She staggered.

speaker: vex
text: "The forest uses names for roots. This isn't the forest."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana stepped forward.

speaker: caelin
text: "Stop listening."

speaker: vex
text: "That's not how ears work,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex snapped, hand moving to a dagger hilt.

speaker: narrator
text: Then it spoke again -- Serana's voice this time, steady and cold.

speaker: caelin
text: "Faith bends. It doesn't always break."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's jaw clenched.

speaker: vex
text: "That's what I said last night."

speaker: nyxara
text: "It remembers,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said.

speaker: vex
text: "It's collecting words that meant something."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik, because curiosity survived even terror, called softly into the dark.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Thornik."

speaker: narrator
text: Nothing.

speaker: narrator
text: He exhaled, relieved -- then heard it whispered back three times in a child's voice, each repetition younger than the last.

speaker: narrator
text: He stumbled back hard enough to knock his goggles askew.

speaker: vex
text: "Never again,"

speaker: narrator
text: he muttered.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Never -- again."

speaker: narrator
text: The air trembled, repeating the words in exact cadence:

speaker: vex
text: "Never again."

speaker: narrator
text: Then quieter:

speaker: nyxara
text: "Never."

speaker: narrator
text: Light warped with the next pulse of the hum. The walls sweated condensation that smelled of iron and oil. Their torches flared green, then white. The flame stretched sideways, casting shadows that now mouthed along with the voices.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana tried to summon her wardlight. The sigil formed, flickered, and folded in on itself until only a faint circle of ash remained.

speaker: vex
text: "I can't hold it,"

speaker: narrator
text: she whispered.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Even faith has an echo."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan drew his axe, slow and steady.

speaker: vex
text: "Forward. Always forward."

speaker: vex
text: "Why not back?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex asked.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Because back isn't there anymore,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, and pointed.

speaker: narrator
text: The corridor behind them had sealed -- not collapsed, but simply wasn't, replaced by seamless metal still warm to the touch.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik stared.

speaker: vex
text: "Topography editing. It's rewriting as we move."

speaker: caelin
text: "Then we move fast,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed once toward the left passage -- hot, insistent. Not the black-violet of corruption, but the bright warm pulse of recognition. Caelin didn't wait for a vote.

speaker: vex
text: "Left,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, and moved.

speaker: vex
text: "Trusting the thing that just tried to eat us?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex asked, falling in behind him.

speaker: caelin
text: "It's not trust,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: vex
text: "It's recognition."

speaker: narrator
text: As they walked, the walls began to whisper fragments of old conversations: Vex mocking Thornik's kettle, Serana's muttered prayers, Elowen's earlier warning about warmth.

speaker: narrator
text: The mountain had been listening since Thornwick. Now it was talking back.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen's hand brushed Caelin's arm.

speaker: caelin
text: "The flame mark answers to more than one master,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: vex
text: "I know,"

speaker: narrator
text: he replied.

speaker: caelin
text: "I just don't know which is winning."

speaker: narrator
text: The corridor opened into another chamber -- a narrow nave lined with glass coffers filled with ash. Light flickered inside each like dying hearts.

speaker: narrator
text: Then his own voice came from the darkness ahead:

speaker: vex
text: "I called the fire. I killed her to save you."

speaker: narrator
text: Every muscle in Caelin's body locked.

speaker: narrator
text: That sentence didn't exist outside his memory. No one else had ever heard it.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana stepped closer, sword half drawn.

speaker: caelin
text: "Who said that?"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin swallowed.

speaker: vex
text: "I did. Once."

speaker: narrator
text: The echo stepped forward -- his height, his face, his wound still glowing faintly through translucent skin.

speaker: narrator
text: It smiled.

speaker: caelin
text: "Still burning,"

speaker: narrator
text: it said.

speaker: narrator
text: He backed away. The others followed his lead, weapons ready but useless against something made of memory.

speaker: narrator
text: The echo tilted its head, movements just slightly off-beat, as if learning how joints worked.

speaker: narrator
text: When it spoke again, it used all their voices together.

speaker: vex
text: "We are the Seal."

speaker: narrator
text: The hum surged; dust fell in bright spirals. The glass coffers flared, each releasing a puff of ash that drifted like breath.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin reached for fire, stopped himself, forced the heat inward until it was pain and not power. The scale burned angry red under his skin but he held it, contained it.

speaker: narrator
text: The mimic paused -- as if confused by restraint -- and in that instant Serana hurled her dagger through its chest.

speaker: narrator
text: The blade passed through light and hit the wall behind with a sharp, clean clink.

speaker: narrator
text: The echo shattered into sound -- hundreds of overlapping syllables that faded into silence.

speaker: narrator
text: When hearing returned, Vex said softly,

speaker: caelin
text: "Next time, we burn it first."

speaker: caelin
text: "No,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: vex
text: "We learn why it knows my name."

speaker: narrator
text: They left the chamber in silence, the scale in Caelin's arm fading from red back to baseline coal-glow.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them, the shards of light pulled together, reforming a faint outline.

speaker: narrator
text: It whispered after them in Caelin's voice, calm and sure:

speaker: caelin
text: "Still burning."

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: They reached the far archway and the space opened like a cathedral of living rock. Three vaulted passages split at precise angles; ribs of stone arced floor to ceiling in calculated load-bearing perfection.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik consulted his journal one last time, marked a route with charcoal.

speaker: vex
text: "Junction chambers. The caldera route is our way out."

speaker: narrator
text: But the ribs were cracking. Visible stress fractures spider-webbing across supports that had held for millennia. Dust sifted in steady streams. The air smelled of ozone and pulverized granite.

speaker: narrator
text: And the sound --

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Deep. Resonant. Felt through boot-soles more than heard.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: The Seal's heartbeat. Not from one direction. From everywhere. The mountain itself had become its chamber.

speaker: thornik
text: "Everyone move,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, voice tight.

speaker: vex
text: "This entire structure is about to -- "

speaker: narrator
text: The mountain shook.

speaker: narrator
text: The junction chamber came apart in stages.

speaker: narrator
text: First: the leftmost rib sheared. Not slowly. Not with warning. Just -- SNAP -- and tons of stone cascaded from the vault in wagon-sized blocks.

speaker: vex
text: "LEFT SIDE!"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex shouted as they scrambled right and the floor where they'd been standing vanished under rubble.

speaker: narrator
text: The mountain's rhythm broke into a riot: THUD--thud--THUD -- thud--THUD, a crowd of heartbeats not their own. Caelin's pulse tried to follow and stumbled; a sick skip behind his sternum knocked his balance half a step off.

speaker: narrator
text: Dust hissed from the ribs like breath through teeth. The air carried the wet-clay breath of new fractures and a metal tang, and static prickled along their forearms.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale in Caelin's arm blazed black-violet at the edges -- full corruption bloom, the Seal's influence flooding the chamber like invisible tide. Not close. Not here. But present. Aware. The obsidian plate pulsed irregular, excited: thud-THUD-thud-THUD.

speaker: narrator
text: It wants me, he understood. The Seal. It can FEEL the Ember. And it's using whatever power it has to collapse the mountain, to drop me closer to it.

speaker: narrator
text: Second: the right-hand arch -- their intended exit -- let go like a builder's curse fulfilled. The pre-stressed keystone clicked, a clean crack ran the span, and the arch folded inward in a slate-gray avalanche that sealed the passage throat to floor.

speaker: narrator
text: Shadows slid ahead of the collapse -- thin whips testing the raw edge where ward-glyphs didn't quite reach. Caelin's scale flared red-gold; its halo seared those probes to curling soot that skittered away like burned spiders.

speaker: narrator
text: Third: the floor cracked. A black lightning zigzag split the chamber from waist to wall and widened with every breath. The Seal's poly-rhythm devolved into pure thrill -- THUD-thud-THUD-thud-THUD -- no pattern, just a chained thing thrashing in joy at the scent of fire.

speaker: narrator
text: ELOWEN was at the rear -- counting heads, keeping stragglers from becoming ghosts -- when the slab under her boots lowered, wrong as a breath drawn by stone. She saw Durgan sprint clear; Nyxara took the widening cut in one clean leap; Caelin turned back toward her --

speaker: narrator
text: Not enough time.

speaker: narrator
text: She chose.

speaker: narrator
text: She slammed her staff and called the new magic: not growth, load-sharing. Roots erupted from living wood, racing across the failing geometry in a glowing web -- but these weren't the sickly pale green of dying nature-magic.

speaker: narrator
text: They blazed emerald shot through with threads of silver-gold, the Abjuration influence from the Deep Hall now woven into her very being.

speaker: narrator
text: The roots didn't brace the mountain; they borrowed weight -- took the load for three breaths, then two, then one -- as the transformed power coursed through her tattoos until the channels screamed.

speaker: narrator
text: The smell of burning green knifed through dust -- sharp chlorophyll-sweet, life paying itself out, but underneath it the metallic tang of ward-magic being consumed.

speaker: narrator
text: The web held. The lowering paused.

speaker: thornik
text: "GO!"

speaker: narrator
text: Her voice cut the riot clean.

speaker: vex
text: "I can't hold -- MOVE!"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's hand went up -- chalk sigil, instinct, geometry she still believed in -- and the ward simply wasn't there. The mountain swallowed the shape before it formed.

speaker: narrator
text: She chose the only thing left: she hooked Caelin's harness with both hands and dragged him back before the slab surged. She chose too: one lost or many. Not a prayer. Just arithmetic.

speaker: narrator
text: He wrenched to go anyway. The floor lurched.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen's tattoos burned from emerald-and-silver to pure white. The root-web flared to ash, spent -- not shattered, not explosive, simply gone. The transformation magic that had given her new purpose consumed itself in one final act of protection.

speaker: narrator
text: The slab dropped. She went with it, swallowed in a grinding maw of stone. No cry. No light -- neither green nor gold. No staff -- wood splintered as the last charge emptied.

speaker: narrator
text: Dust boiled and sealed the black mouth into a gray scar.

speaker: narrator
text: Above, the riot in the rock pitched higher, delighted. Caelin lunged for the edge --

speaker: nyxara
text: "MOVE OR -- "

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara started, a warning that became prophecy as their section lowered under their boots.

speaker: narrator
text: They fell.

speaker: narrator
text: One heartbeat: solid stone. The next: nothing. Caelin tumbled through choking dust, the world a roll of jagged edges and red sparks. Instinct screamed heat the air -- stupid instinct. Hot wind doesn't hold weight.

speaker: narrator
text: He tucked, took the rock on shoulder and thigh, let pain be ballast. A debris slope caught him -- cruel, angled, still better than a pit -- and he shed momentum in skin and blood.

speaker: narrator
text: Parallel on the slide, Nyxara fell with the eerie grace of someone who'd made a career out of surviving bad ideas. Pact-power whispered -- no force, no shove -- simply convinced the falling stones she wasn't part of the story.

speaker: narrator
text: Angles and experience did the rest. She surfed the avalanche to a jutting ledge and cut speed in a low, bruising crouch.

speaker: narrator
text: They hit. Caelin sprawled, gasping; his right arm lit in a lattice of red pain as the scale jarred against bone. The filaments flared angry red, screaming, then slowly faded back toward coal-glow.

speaker: narrator
text: Above, stone sealed on stone.

speaker: vex
text: "CAELIN!"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's voice knifed through muffling tons.

speaker: nyxara
text: "CAN YOU HEAR ME?"

speaker: narrator
text: He coughed grit.

speaker: vex
text: "ALIVE! WE'RE ALIVE!"

speaker: nyxara
text: "CAN'T REACH YOU! ROUTE'S SEALED!"

speaker: vex
text: "WE'LL FIND ANOTHER WAY!"

speaker: nyxara
text: "SURFACE EXIT -- EAST FACE -- WE'LL WAIT!"

speaker: narrator
text: Thin as thread, then gone. Either distance, or the mountain decided they'd said enough.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin sat, cataloguing damage: bruises blooming, shoulder shrieking, the lattice aching but holding. He hadn't cast. Could have. Wanted to. Didn't. Small victory.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara tested joints -- grimace, then a shrug.

speaker: vex
text: "Sprained. Mobile. Ten out of ten would not recommend."

speaker: narrator
text: He stood, staggered, found purpose.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Mark the wall. Let them know."

speaker: narrator
text: He burned Draconic into a clean slab with a tight ember thread: ALIVE. FOLLOWING HEAT. FIND US SURFACE EAST.

speaker: narrator
text: Beneath it, he added Thornik's party sigil -- three short strokes, one long. So the others would know it was them.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale's glow dimmed from ember-red back to soft coal as the magic settled.

speaker: narrator
text: Somewhere beyond the sealed chokes and ruined ribs, the noise shifted. The riot dropped back to patience; the world's pressure exhaled.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Not below, not above -- everywhere. Presence, not location. Influence bleeding through leagues of stone.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Time to move,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said, already limping toward the only path that wasn't trying to kill them yet.

speaker: vex
text: "Before the mountain changes its mind. Or the pets find a new seam."

speaker: narrator
text: He stood there a moment longer than the task required.

speaker: narrator
text: He kept thinking of small things. The way she'd pressed two fingers to stone and said it listens back. The first time she eased the lattice-burn and he'd been too surprised to say thank you.

speaker: narrator
text: The vine-marks on her wrists the morning they left the Deep Hall -- two more blackened, and she hadn't mentioned it, just kept walking. He hadn't mentioned it either. He'd thought there was time.

speaker: narrator
text: That was the part that would stay with him. Not the sound of the slab. The assumption of time.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin looked once at the sealed scar. No token. No miracle. The stone didn't care, and he said it anyway:

speaker: nyxara
text: "I'm sorry."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale warmed -- acknowledgment, not comfort.

speaker: narrator
text: He put her name away in the place where you put things you can't carry and can't put down. Then he turned into the dark.

speaker: narrator
text: Up in the junction they'd abandoned, dust settled where green-and-gold light had been. No voice. No staff. No trace.

speaker: narrator
text: The mountain's riot eased back to patient counting, as if the chain below had tested the links and found them holding -- for now.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan stood at the edge of the sealed passage, ice-blue eyes tracking the crack where Caelin had disappeared. His shadow had leapt for that crack like a starving animal smelling meat -- reaching, stretching ten feet across rubble before he crushed it flat with will and pain.

speaker: narrator
text: The pressure burst something. Blood ran from both nostrils now, not a trickle but a stream. He wiped it with the back of his hand, stared at the red smear, then tucked his hand away.

speaker: narrator
text: Faster, he thought. It's happening faster.

speaker: narrator
text: No one had seen. They were too busy staring at where their companions had fallen.

speaker: serana
text: "We move,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, voice hollow but functional.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Find another route. Reach the surface. Meet them at the east exit."

speaker: narrator
text: She didn't say if they're alive. The silence did it for her.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik unrolled his journal with shaking hands.

speaker: serana
text: "Vent shaft route. Steep. Narrow. But it reaches surface."

speaker: narrator
text: He marked it with charcoal, the line wavering.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Two hours. Maybe three."

speaker: vex
text: "Then we climb,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said. Her voice was flat, empty of its usual edge. She pressed a hand to her ribs, felt them creak, and kept it there. She turned to move -- and caught the tail end of Durgan tucking his hand into his sleeve. Not fast enough. The wrong colour on his upper lip.

speaker: narrator
text: She held the look for one breath. Filed it. Said nothing. Elowen was gone and Caelin was somewhere below and her ribs were broken and there was only so much room.

speaker: narrator
text: They turned toward the vent shaft entrance -- a crack in the chamber wall barely wide enough for shoulders.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them, the Seal counted.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Patient. Waiting.

speaker: narrator
text: The rubble-slope fell away for what felt like miles. In the dark, distance turned elastic, measured only by the steady coal-glow of Caelin's scale and the pricked red points far below.

speaker: narrator
text: He counted steps so he wouldn't count losses. One breath. One more. Again.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale sat at coal-glow -- baseline, steady, indifferent to grief. He focused on that. On the temperature of the stone under his boots, the pull of the debris slope, the small calculations of footing. He was good at small calculations.

speaker: narrator
text: He had been making them since Thornwick and they had kept him useful and upright and he was going to keep making them now, in this tunnel, in this dark, until the habit of surviving outlasted the habit of feeling.

speaker: narrator
text: It wasn't working especially well.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara moved beside him with careful economy despite the twist in her ankle, saying nothing for long stretches, then:

speaker: nyxara
text: "You're quiet. Brooding, or rationing air?"

speaker: vex
text: "Thinking,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: nyxara
text: "About the druid who bought us time."

speaker: narrator
text: No softness to it. Just truth.

speaker: vex
text: "Yeah."

speaker: nyxara
text: "She chose,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said.

speaker: vex
text: "You honor it by surviving, not by drowning in guilt."

speaker: narrator
text: A beat.

speaker: nyxara
text: "And by keeping that thing in your arm from getting us both killed."

speaker: narrator
text: They dropped onto a broader landing. Caelin paused, burned a quick line on the wall: ALIVE ? and beneath it the simple four-stroke sigil Thornik had taught them -- three short, one long.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale's glow brightened briefly as he worked, then settled back to baseline coal.

speaker: narrator
text: They moved again. The air warmed by degrees until the tunnel opened like a throat into a chamber so vast it made their footfalls feel impertinent.

speaker: narrator
text: The Forge Caldera wasn't a room; it was a memory made of stone.

speaker: narrator
text: Tiered galleries ringed a polished obsidian floor. Dozens of cold hearths stood in concentric circles around a central pit, their banks still faintly embered as if the world had simply forgotten to finish cooling.

speaker: narrator
text: The place smelled of quenched steel and old oil; somewhere in the rock a thin, impossible ringing went on, like the last note of a hammer that never quite died.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin's Ember answered with a low pulse; his scale brightened from coal to ember-red -- not warning, but recognition. Bright warm pulse of resonance.

speaker: nyxara
text: "This is where they forged the Concord,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara murmured, actual awe loosening her voice.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin nodded, chest tight. Heritage felt like grief when it came late.

speaker: narrator
text: Across the caldera, a bridge arched over the central pit -- elegant, dwarven, impossible. Beyond it, stairs spiraled toward a pale wash of daylight.

speaker: vex
text: "Route's there,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: nyxara
text: "We skirt the pit."

speaker: narrator
text: They set out along the perimeter, keeping the corrupted glow at center-left and plenty of stone between. It wasn't the black-violet ache of the Seal; it was old taint, a bruise that never healed.

speaker: narrator
text: Halfway around, Nyxara's footing went soft on a talus spill and she started to slide. She caught herself on a carved rail, hissed at the jolt, then whispered something that wasn't words so much as suggestion.

speaker: narrator
text: Her pact-whisper slid across the debris -- not force but permission -- and the fall obliged, choosing other paths. She righted, breath steadying.

speaker: narrator
text: Around them, the scale showed a brief silver shimmer -- proximity to active magic -- then faded back.

speaker: caelin
text: "Efficient,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Persuasive,"

speaker: narrator
text: she corrected, amused.

speaker: caelin
text: "My specialty."

speaker: narrator
text: They reached the bridge. On the rail, half-buried in dust, lay a fractured tool: a joint-mark chisel with dwarvish geometry on one cheek and a draconic maker-sigil on the other -- a language of edges and fire.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin picked it up, wiped grime with his sleeve, and tucked it into his pack.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Proof,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, thinking of Thornik.

speaker: caelin
text: "For the councils who don't believe."

speaker: narrator
text: On the near abutment, he burned the party sigil and a neat ? EAST arrow into the stone.

speaker: nyxara
text: "In case they come this way,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara limped onto the span, then paused to breathe.

speaker: caelin
text: "Not to be indelicate,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, tone going bright with mischief,

speaker: nyxara
text: "but we're alone, nearly died, and you still haven't tried to hold my hand for courage. Should I be insulted?"

speaker: caelin
text: "Flirting seems inefficient,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said, and meant it.

speaker: narrator
text: She laughed -- genuine, quick.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Impossible to embarrass. Refreshing."

speaker: narrator
text: She patted his shoulder, friendly, not coy.

speaker: caelin
text: "I'll keep trying anyway. Everyone breaks eventually."

speaker: nyxara
text: "I don't think I'm going to."

speaker: caelin
text: "That's what makes it interesting."

speaker: narrator
text: They crossed without looking down. The staircase beyond breathed knife-cold air flecked with the mineral tang of altitude. Daylight brightened from rumor to promise.

speaker: narrator
text: They climbed, and the caldera's endless ringing faded behind them like a heartbeat choosing to be memory.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Distant. Patient. Still counting.

speaker: narrator
text: At the final landing, a door waited -- dwarf-carved, sealed by ice grown old enough to be stubborn. Caelin set two fingers of heat to the latch: just enough to soften, not enough to cost. The mechanism sighed; the slab rolled aside.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed angry red briefly -- small magic, small cost -- then settled back.

speaker: narrator
text: Daylight. Sky. A ledge chiseled into the mountain's east face.

speaker: narrator
text: The air was knife-cold and clean, spiced with sun-warmed lichen and far pine-smoke dragged upslope by a contrary breeze.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara closed her eyes and stood in it like a priest.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Surface,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: caelin
text: "I had a bet with myself."

speaker: nyxara
text: "You win?"

speaker: caelin
text: "I always do."

speaker: narrator
text: They didn't linger. Caelin oriented -- sun angle, ridge line, memory of shouted directions through stone.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Forge Road exit should be two hours along this band."

speaker: nyxara
text: "Then let's not give the mountain time to change its mind,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said, and they set off along the ledge, the Emberpeaks glowering polite and ancient behind them.

speaker: narrator
text: The vent shaft was a stone throat that refused to swallow.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik went first, shoulders scraping both walls, muttering cheerfully obscene things at geology. Vex slid after him sideways, breath clipped to keep her ribs from complaining out loud. Serana followed, armor ghosting sparks from old rock.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan brought up the rear, dragging nothing but a long, obedient shadow.

speaker: narrator
text: Grit rained in fits. Serana laced a paper-thin ward over their faces -- muscle memory, not miracle -- and it skinned the dust from their eyes when the shaft shed a handful of centuries. The light was hers -- steady gold from trained discipline, not divine gift -- and it held.

speaker: thornik
text: "Remind me,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik grunted, wedging through a pinch,

speaker: nyxara
text: "why I volunteered the route most likely to make me a cork."

speaker: vex
text: "Because the alternative involved dying beautifully underground,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said. She tried a small breath; her ribs sparked protest. She hid the wince behind a scowl.

speaker: nyxara
text: "You also said, and I quote, 'Left shaft has the coldest air -- surface that way.'"

speaker: vex
text: "Present-Thornik applauds Past-Thornik's olfactory heroism,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, pleased despite himself.

speaker: narrator
text: They squeezed into a run of broader tunnel, air colder, cleaner. Frost rimed old soot on the stone. Above them: a ribbon of blue like a promise.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana climbed on discipline more than faith. Her goddess had been a static-laced channel for days; the old prayers returned as faint as wishful thinking. Fine. Duty would do. Duty and the ward under her skin, thin as paper and twice as stubborn.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind, Durgan stopped once, hand braced to the wall. He wiped his nose with the back of his hand -- checking, confirming -- but the skin came away clean.

speaker: narrator
text: Not yet, he thought. But soon.

speaker: narrator
text: He could feel it building. The intervals between shadow-tests were shortening. What had been hours was now minutes. He dug his nails into his palm until pain returned his hands to him, then climbed on.

speaker: narrator
text: At a three-way split, Thornik sniffed the drafts, checked his grandfather's blood-spattered diagram, and pointed left.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Coldest. And it tastes like snow,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, delighted.

speaker: vex
text: "Your nose is a menace,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, grimacing as she tried to keep pace. The ribs pulled with every breath.

speaker: thornik
text: "It's also getting us out,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, gentler than the banter wanted to be.

speaker: narrator
text: They climbed another hour, then the shaft widened to a slot of white.

speaker: narrator
text: The foursome spilled onto a ledge scraped by old avalanches, the world dropping away in a dignified hurry.

speaker: narrator
text: Sky. Actual, indifferent sky.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik shaded his eyes, traced the east face.

speaker: vex
text: "There -- half-hidden by scree. The primary Forge Road exit. If they made it through the forges, they'll come out there."

speaker: serana
text: "How far?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana asked.

speaker: vex
text: "Two hours along the band. Maybe less if the goats have been generous."

speaker: serana
text: "Then we move,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said. She looked once at the mountain, where the wind carved the ridges into saints and knives, and whispered the two words she hadn't let herself say aloud:

speaker: vex
text: "For Elowen."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's hand found her dagger hilt, white-knuckled. She said nothing. Didn't need to.

speaker: narrator
text: They started the traverse, Durgan last, shadow flat and patient at his heels.

speaker: narrator
text: Below: Caelin and Nyxara hiked the east-face band, the ledge slanting toward a darker cleft that had to be the exit. Nyxara kept to his left -- habit now, where the drop yawned wider -- and made a show of not leaning on him.

speaker: serana
text: "Almost there,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: vex
text: "See? No dramatic rescues required. Very dull."

speaker: serana
text: "Would've helped if you'd fallen,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, then realized how it sounded.

speaker: vex
text: "To get it over with."

speaker: narrator
text: He grimaced.

speaker: serana
text: "That was poorly phrased."

speaker: vex
text: "Adorable,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, delighted.

speaker: serana
text: "Points for honesty."

speaker: narrator
text: They rounded a buttress; the ledge widened.

speaker: narrator
text: Above: Thornik and Vex took first watch positions overlooking the cave mouth. The air bit their teeth; the lichen patches on sun-facing rock bled a pleasant, warm smell that didn't belong to cold places.

speaker: vex
text: "They're late,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, hand pressed to her ribs.

speaker: thornik
text: "They're alive,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik countered, because you had to choose which truth to feed.

speaker: narrator
text: Below: A glint of metal ahead -- a buckle on a pack strap -- then the shape wearing it resolved into Thornik, broader than the mountain remembered.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin lifted a hand.

speaker: narrator
text: Above:

speaker: thornik
text: "Movement,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, and Vex was already rising, jaw tight as the ribs reminded her she was made of meat and stubbornness.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana stood slower, as if unwilling to jinx it, and Durgan's eyes found a coal-glow he'd been both dreading and needing.

speaker: narrator
text: Below: Nyxara waved like they'd come across friends at a festival.

speaker: vex
text: "Well. How convenient."

speaker: narrator
text: Above:

speaker: serana
text: "They're alive,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, and didn't smile so much as allow the expression to happen to her face.

speaker: narrator
text: They met on a rubble-shelf wide enough for six people and too small for grief.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's gauntleted hand settled on Caelin's shoulder -- not a hug, just proof.

speaker: vex
text: "You made it."

speaker: serana
text: "So did you,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik checked them both with brisk, professional hands, then blinked when Caelin handed him the fractured chisel. He turned it over, breath catching at the twin maker's marks -- dwarf and dragon, working as one.

speaker: vex
text: "By the old forges,"

speaker: narrator
text: he whispered.

speaker: serana
text: "This... this is going to shut some very loud people up."

speaker: caelin
text: "Use it well,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex hung back a half step, letting relief show only in how her knives went away without being asked. She straightened too fast; pain shot through her ribs. She swallowed the sound.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik noticed anyway and shifted his pack off his right shoulder so she wouldn't have to take the wind on her bad side.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara's eyes moved across faces, counting.

speaker: serana
text: "Not everyone,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, soft for her.

speaker: narrator
text: No one answered. The mountain had already said it.

speaker: narrator
text: From the stone's long throat, distant and patient and impossible to place: thud. Thud. Thud. The Seal's count resumed, indifferent to daylight.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana let the moment last exactly as long as it could bear and not a blink more.

speaker: caelin
text: "We move. Millbrook is three days. Rest, resupply, information. Then we plan."

speaker: thornik
text: "What do we plan?"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik asked, though he knew.

speaker: caelin
text: "To finish what we started,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: thornik
text: "To make it mean something."

speaker: narrator
text: They turned down-slope, six where there had been seven, the east wind combing the cliff grass into silver.

speaker: narrator
text: Half a switchback below, Vex paused, head canted to the wind.

speaker: caelin
text: "Don't look now,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said lightly,

speaker: thornik
text: "but we have admirers."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik followed her line. Far down the face, where the scree ran like a river, a single figure crested a rib of stone.

speaker: narrator
text: Too still. Too patient. Even at distance, something about its silhouette felt WRONG -- the way it held itself, the way it didn't shift weight like living things do.

speaker: narrator
text: It watched.

speaker: narrator
text: Then it stepped backward -- not turning, just REVERSING -- and was gone.

speaker: serana
text: "Scouts,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, voice flat. Not afraid. Accounting.

speaker: thornik
text: "Distant,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex confirmed, hand already on a dagger hilt.

speaker: serana
text: "For now."

speaker: serana
text: "Then we take the gift of distance,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, and set them moving again.

speaker: narrator
text: They left the mountain to its long arithmetic -- counting, waiting, patient as chains -- and followed a narrower math: miles to town, hours to water, steps to sleep.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them, the Forge Road's cave mouth went back to being rock. The junction chambers settled into their new geometry. And somewhere vast and deep, something chained counted heartbeats that weren't its own.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Ahead, the sky was very blue and very cruel and very free.

speaker: narrator
text: END CHAPTER 7

speaker: narrator
